message:
fix(fcp-streaming/websocket): clamp WsConfig::max_message_size at 16 MiB ceiling (br-frvd2)

Close the memory-amplification hole in the WebSocket client config:
any caller that passed a large (or attacker-controlled) value to
WsConfig::with_max_message_size() would have that value sent straight
to tungstenite's WebSocketConfig::max_message_size, letting a single
inbound message allocate up to `size` bytes before the handler saw a
single byte. usize::MAX was accepted literally, which on a 64-bit
host means the server could request an allocation up to ~16 EiB per
message and crash the process.

## Fix: hard ceiling via MAX_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_SIZE

Introduce a pub const at module level:

    pub const MAX_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_SIZE: usize = 16 * 1024 * 1024;

16 MiB covers every legitimate WebSocket traffic pattern the streaming
subsystem handles (binary frames, large JSON payloads, fragmented
text) with headroom to spare. Any caller that wants to inject a
larger value has lost the argument — the ceiling is applied at three
places so there's no bypass:

  1. `websocket_config(&WsConfig)` helper (used internally on every
     connect to build the tungstenite config) — clamps with
     `config.max_message_size.min(MAX_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_SIZE)`.

  2. `WsConfig::default()` — sets max_message_size to
     MAX_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_SIZE (down from 64 MiB). The previous
     64 MiB default was above the new ceiling; nobody should be
     surprised that the ceiling is the default.

  3. `WsConfig::with_max_message_size(size)` — non-const if clamp
     at the setter so callers that inspect `config.max_message_size`
     after setting see the effective value, not the requested one:

         pub const fn with_max_message_size(mut self, size: usize) -> Self {
             self.max_message_size = if size > MAX_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_SIZE {
                 MAX_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_SIZE
             } else {
                 size
             };
             self
         }

  The early-branch form is used because const fn doesn't support
  `usize::min` in stable Rust at the pinned MSRV.

## Regression tests

  - ws_config_max_message_size_large: pre-fix passed usize::MAX through
    and asserted `config.max_message_size == usize::MAX`. Now asserts
    it equals MAX_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_SIZE — flipping this test is the
    behavior change under review.

  - The default-config test at ws_config_default() updates its
    expected max_message_size from `64 * 1024 * 1024` to
    MAX_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_SIZE so the test expectation stays in sync
    with the actual default.

## Beads

Transition flywheel_connectors-frvd2 from `open` -> `in_progress`.
This commit addresses the bead; the closure will land in the next
sweep after the streaming team verifies no downstream caller was
relying on the higher ceiling (grep-based audit didn't find any in
the tracked tree).

Tracks br-frvd2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fnzgx","title":"[tooling] rch worker nightly drift triggers local fail-open after crate-scoped remote cargo","description":"Background: after the earlier RCH-E326 dependency-cycle work, crate-scoped offload from crates/fcp-kernel is now viable enough to reach remote cargo. On 2026-03-30, rch exec -- cargo check --lib from crates/fcp-kernel prepared 11 roots, finished remote sync in about 20.8 seconds, and started remote cargo on worker vmi1149989. That remote run then failed because the worker lacked toolchain nightly-2026-02-18-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, after which rch attempted to fail open locally. In shared multi-agent sessions this local fallback is forbidden because it can start unplanned local compilation storms. Scope: track this as a distinct tooling blocker so agents can separate it from the older path-cycle issue and choose safer verification paths. Acceptance: 1) the failure mode is explicitly tracked in beads, 2) future agents can distinguish worker-nightly drift plus local fail-open from repo metadata cycles, 3) next steps are clear whether the remedy belongs in worker fleet maintenance, rch worker selection, or repo-local guidance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:13:50.105649Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-31T02:43:56.290458Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T02:43:56.290044Z","close_reason":"Documented rch worker-nightly drift, retrieval-only failure, and unsafe local fail-open handling in AGENTS.md and README.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fqzmp","title":"fcp-mesh/node: planner zones never populated (production zone-policy filtering broken)","description":"**File**: crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:792, 801

**Code**:
```rust
fn build_planner_input(&self, now_ms: u64) -> PlannerInput {
    if let Some(profile) = &self.local_profile {
        nodes.push(NodeInfo {
            ...
            zones: Vec::new(), // TODO: populate from mesh enrollment data
        });
    }
    for state in self.peers.values() {
        nodes.push(NodeInfo {
            ...
            zones: Vec::new(), // TODO: populate from peer zone advertisements
        });
    }
}
```

**Root cause**: `MeshNode::build_planner_input` hardcodes `zones: Vec::new()` for both the local node and every peer. The TODOs mark this as never being wired to real enrollment data or peer advertisements.

**Impact**: `PlannerInput → NodeInfo.zones` is threaded into `CandidateNode.zones` (planner.rs:537). Planner zone-policy filtering (planner.rs:1852 `zone_policy_rejects_nodes_with_unknown_zones`, 1806 `zone_policy_filters_candidates_by_zone`) keys off this field. In production, every candidate has empty zones, so:
- `zone_policy` set to allow only `z:work` rejects ALL candidates (no node has any zone)
- `zone_policy` set to deny `z:public` allows ALL candidates (no node is in z:public from the policy's view)
- The planner cannot enforce zone-affinity placement at all

This is silently broken: no error, no warning, just universally-bypassed filtering.

**Fix plan**:
1. Plumb local zones from the existing MeshIdentity / MeshConfig source-of-truth (likely `identity.tags` filtered through ZoneTagMapping::tag_to_zone) into local_profile or a sibling local_zones field on MeshNode.
2. Track per-peer zones from the gossip / handshake path. Peers already advertise tags via their MeshIdentity in handshake; add zone derivation alongside symbol-summary handling.
3. Replace both `zones: Vec::new()` sites with the real source.
4. Add an integration test in fcp-mesh: build_planner_input populates non-empty zones for local node when MeshIdentity has fcp tags; peer state with attested fcp tags appears in planner candidates with matching zones.
5. Add a planner-level test that demonstrates zone-policy filtering actually filters once zones flow through correctly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:39:48.973287Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:21:17.298362Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:21:17.297599Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit a75b9e9d: fcp-mesh/node thread local and peer zones through build_planner_input","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2798,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fqzmp","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fix landed in a75b9e9d by another swarm agent — independently arrived at the same design (local_zones HashSet on MeshNode, update_local_zones setter, both build_planner_input sites wired, same regression test shape). Verified via CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p1-t3 rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-mesh --lib plan_execution_populates_zones_from_local_and_peer_state (1 passed).","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:21:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fr3x5","title":"Implement huggingface connector: model inference and hub API","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:14:10.605019Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:50:02.974513Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:50:02.974345Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","hardening","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fr3x5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1i9ky","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:14:20.587822Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1963,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fr3x5","author":"jemanuel","text":"HuggingFace Hub + Inference API. Well-documented at https://huggingface.co/docs/api-inference.

Key operations: model inference (text-generation, summarization, etc.), model listing, dataset listing. The scaffold declares these. Implementation follows the standard connector pattern: HTTP client, typed requests/responses, ConnectorErrorMapping, wiremock tests.

API key auth via Authorization: Bearer hf_... header.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:14:20Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-frvd2","title":"fcp-streaming: websocket config can disable payload-size cap","description":"crates/fcp-streaming/src/websocket.rs:35, 439-440, 1474-1476: with_max_message_size accepts usize::MAX and connect_websocket forwards it into the transport builder, so the peer-facing frame-size ceiling can be disabled.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:09:57.212554Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:09:57.212554Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ftw","title":"fcp.reddit: Saved Items Management","description":"# fcp.reddit: Saved Items Management (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow agents to manage saved posts/comments for research workflows:
- save/unsave
- list saved items

## Scope
- Save a post/comment.
- Unsave a post/comment.
- List saved items (pagination).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.saved.read`
- `reddit.saved.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Receipts/audit for write operations.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.saved.list`
  - `reddit.saved.save`
  - `reddit.saved.unsave`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - save/unsave idempotency
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Saved-items workflows are reliable and auditable.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:22.499785716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:59:39.517687Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:57:53.871469Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ftw","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-xz6e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ftw","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-xz6e.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1367,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ftw","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented saved.rs: SavedItems pagination, Save/Unsave with fullname validation, SavedItemsPolicy, Unicode-safe body preview. 143 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:57:49Z"},{"id":1369,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ftw","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented saved.list (GET /user/{username}/saved), saved.save (POST /api/save), saved.unsave (POST /api/unsave). Full saved items lifecycle. Reddit at 22 ops, tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:59:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","title":"fcp.airtable: Attachment upload and download handling","description":"# fcp.airtable: Attachment Upload and Download Handling

## Goal
Handle Airtable attachments safely under strict sandboxing.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.attachments.upload`
- `airtable.attachments.get` (fetch attachment metadata/URL)
- optional: `airtable.attachments.download` (if we choose to implement actual download)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write separated
- NetworkConstraints:
  - Airtable API host(s) allowed
  - attachment download hosts must be explicitly enumerated/pinned
  - validate URLs are HTTPS and match allowlist
- Data handling:
  - attachments move as **ObjectId references** (no local disk persistence)
  - enforce size limits and streaming bounds
- Logging:
  - never log attachment contents
  - URLs may contain opaque tokens; treat as sensitive

## Tests
- Allow/deny matrix for attachment URL hosts.
- Size limit and timeout tests.
- Redaction tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Attachment flows are safe-by-default and deterministic.
- Non-allowed hosts are blocked by Network Guard.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"MossyShore","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:14:35.521843538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.629767Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.629752Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1094,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","author":"MossyShore","text":"Implemented Airtable attachment download hardening. Changes: enforce HTTPS/host/port validation against the manifest allowlist, walk redirects manually with per-hop validation, cap response size at 100 MiB before/while reading, and stop logging token-bearing attachment URLs via tracing/error messages. Added focused tests for disallowed hosts, oversized downloads, local redirect handling, and FCP error mapping. Validation completed: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-check cargo check -p fcp-airtable --all-targets; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-test cargo test -p fcp-airtable --test integration -- --nocapture; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-clippy cargo clippy -p fcp-airtable --all-targets -- -D warnings. Workspace-wide cargo check was attempted via rch but fell back locally and is still blocked/contended by concurrent cargo activity from other agents, so I am leaving the bead in_progress until that clears or someone explicitly accepts the package-scoped verification.","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:50:36Z"},{"id":1306,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented attachments.rs module: Attachment struct, AttachmentConfig (size/type limits), AttachmentValidation (URL/filename/MIME validation). 110 tests, all passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fytk7","title":"fcp-tailscale exports MockTailscaleClient from production lib surface","description":"`crates/fcp-tailscale/src/lib.rs` publicly reexports `MockTailscaleClient`, and `crates/fcp-tailscale/src/client.rs` declares it as a normal `pub struct` with public constructors/helpers. The only consumers are crate-local unit tests and `crates/fcp-tailscale/tests/no_mock_integration.rs`, so downstream production code can import a pure in-memory fake client today with no explicit test-only gate.

Suggested fix: add an explicit `test-mocks` feature, gate `MockTailscaleClient` and its `lib.rs` reexport behind `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-mocks"))]`, and require that feature for the integration test target so production builds no longer expose the mock surface by default.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:34:15.215012Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:40:28.943793Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:40:28.943564Z","close_reason":"Default builds no longer export MockTailscaleClient; no_mock_integration now opts in via test-mocks","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp-tailscale","security","test-hygiene"]}
